item_a.name: NordVPN
item_a.image_alt: NordVPN logo and app interface on desktop and mobile
pros: Consistently faster speeds thanks to proprietary NordLynx protocol
9,000+ servers across 130+ countries — one of the largest networks
Based in Panama, outside Five Eyes surveillance alliance
Four audited plans including Threat Protection Pro and Double VPN specialty servers
cons: More expensive than PIA — starts at $3.39/mo on a 2-year plan
Highest-tier plans (Prime at $7.39/mo) add significant cost for extras
item_a.best_for: Users who want top-tier speeds, reliable streaming unblocking, and the strongest possible privacy jurisdiction.

item_b.name: Private Internet Access
item_b.image_alt: Private Internet Access VPN logo and minimalist app interface
pros: One of the most affordable premium VPNs — as low as $2.03/mo on long-term plans
Unlimited simultaneous device connections on every plan
35,000+ servers including coverage in all 50 U.S. states
Fully open-source apps and no-logs policy proven in real U.S. court cases
cons: Based in the US, a Five Eyes member country — a theoretical privacy concern
Speeds on long-distance servers can be inconsistent compared to NordVPN
Struggles to unblock some streaming platforms like Amazon Prime Video
item_b.best_for: Budget-conscious users, large households with many devices, and tech-savvy privacy advocates who want open-source transparency.

comparison_summary: NordVPN edges out Private Internet Access in speed, streaming reliability, and privacy jurisdiction, making it the better all-around VPN for most users. That said, PIA's lower price, unlimited device connections, and court-proven no-logs policy make it an excellent pick for budget-driven users and large households. If you can justify the modest price difference, NordVPN delivers a more polished and consistently faster experience.
comparison_intro: The NordVPN vs Private Internet Access debate is one of the most hotly contested matchups in the VPN space — both are affordable, audited, and genuinely privacy-focused. Should you choose NordVPN or Private Internet Access? The answer depends heavily on what you're prioritizing, so let's break it down. Which is better, NordVPN or Private Internet Access, really comes down to three things: speed, price, and how much the jurisdiction of your VPN provider matters to you. The key difference between NordVPN and Private Internet Access is that NordVPN trades slightly higher prices for faster speeds and a Panama base, while PIA compared to NordVPN wins on cost and device flexibility.
verdict_text: NordVPN is the stronger all-around VPN over Private Internet Access, thanks to faster speeds, a more privacy-friendly jurisdiction, and a more reliable streaming experience.

key_differences.item_b: 35,000+ servers in 91 countries, all 50 U.S. states

key_differences.item_a: Consistently fast via NordLynx protocol; minimal speed drop over long distances
key_differences.item_b: Fast on nearby servers; up to 61% speed drop on long-distance connections

key_differences.item_a: Panama — outside Five Eyes, no data retention laws
key_differences.item_b: United States — Five Eyes member country

key_differences.item_a: Audited 4 consecutive years by Deloitte; no real-world legal test
key_differences.item_b: Independently audited; no-logs claim upheld in multiple U.S. court cases

key_differences.item_a: Reliably unblocks Netflix, Prime Video, and most major platforms
key_differences.item_b: Good with Netflix; struggled to unblock Amazon Prime Video in testing

key_differences.item_a: Closed-source apps; audited by third parties
key_differences.item_b: Fully open-source apps available on GitHub

seo_content:
PIA has one trick NordVPN can't match: its no-logs policy has been tested not just by auditors, but by federal courts. When the FBI came knocking, PIA handed over nothing — because there was nothing to hand over. That's a level of real-world validation that even NordVPN's four consecutive Deloitte audits can't directly replicate.

But here's the thing — NordVPN vs Private Internet Access isn't really a privacy debate. Both are trustworthy. The gap is everywhere else.

On speed, NordVPN wins decisively. Its proprietary NordLynx protocol — built on WireGuard but optimized for Nord's infrastructure — keeps speed drops minimal even when tunneling through servers on the other side of the world. PIA, using standard WireGuard, can shed up to 61% of your base speed on long-distance server connections. For day-to-day browsing that's invisible, but for 4K streaming or low-latency gaming, it matters.

Streaming is where Private Internet Access vs NordVPN becomes most lopsided. NordVPN unblocked every major platform I threw at it during testing — Netflix, Disney+, BBC iPlayer, and Amazon Prime Video without a hiccup. PIA handles Netflix well but has a documented problem with Prime Video. If streaming geo-restricted content is your main use case, that gap is hard to overlook.

Pricing flips the script. PIA starts at just $2.03/month on its longest plan, and every feature is unlocked regardless of tier — unlimited devices, split tunneling, port forwarding, the works. NordVPN compared to PIA costs nearly 70% more on a comparable long-term plan, and its most useful extras (like Threat Protection Pro) are gated behind pricier tiers.

Device limits are also worth calling out. NordVPN caps you at 10 simultaneous connections, which is fine for most households. But PIA's unlimited device policy is genuinely rare in the VPN space and makes it an obvious choice for families or anyone securing a router, smart TV, tablet, phone, laptop, and desktop all at once.

One underrated edge for PIA: it has servers in all 50 U.S. states, which is a niche but real advantage for accessing out-of-market sports broadcasts or local content tied to a specific state. NordVPN covers more countries overall but doesn't offer that granular U.S. coverage.

I'd pick NordVPN for most people — particularly anyone who streams a lot, values consistent performance, or wants a VPN that's dead simple to configure with sensible defaults. For privacy purists on a budget managing a house full of devices, PIA is a harder deal to pass up than its reputation sometimes suggests.

faq.q: Is NordVPN better than Private Internet Access?
faq.a: For most users, yes. NordVPN delivers faster speeds, more reliable streaming unblocking, and a stronger privacy jurisdiction (Panama vs. the U.S.). That said, Private Internet Access wins on price and unlimited device connections, so the best choice depends on your priorities.

faq.q: Should I choose NordVPN or Private Internet Access?
faq.a: Choose NordVPN if you stream frequently, game online, or want the fastest and most reliable performance out of the box. Choose Private Internet Access if you're on a tight budget, have many devices to protect, or want a fully open-source VPN with a court-proven no-logs policy.

faq.q: What is the difference between NordVPN and Private Internet Access?
faq.a: The four biggest differences are: (1) NordVPN is based in Panama (outside Five Eyes) while PIA is U.S.-based; (2) NordVPN is consistently faster, especially over long distances; (3) PIA costs roughly 40% less on comparable long-term plans; and (4) PIA allows unlimited simultaneous device connections while NordVPN caps at 10.

faq.q: Does Private Internet Access work with Netflix?
faq.a: Yes, PIA works reliably with Netflix and can unblock regional libraries. However, it has struggled in testing to unblock Amazon Prime Video, where NordVPN performs more consistently.

faq.q: Is Private Internet Access safe even though it's based in the US?
faq.a: PIA's U.S. jurisdiction is a theoretical concern, but its no-logs policy has been upheld in multiple actual court cases where federal authorities subpoenaed user data and PIA had nothing to provide. Its apps are also fully open-source, which is a significant transparency advantage over most competitors.
